content.problemStatement:
Given an integer k and a string s, any character in the string can be selected and changed to any other uppercase English character. This operation can be performed up to k times. After completing these steps, return 
the length of the longest substring that contains the same letter.
constraints: s consists only of uppercase English letters
content.inputFormat: s = string of uppercase English letters, k = integer
content.outputFormat: length of the longest substring that contains the same letter

sampleTestCases.explanation: We can change the B at index 0 and 3 (0-based indexing) to A. The new string becomes "AAAAAABBBAAA". The substring "AAAAAA" is the longest substring with the same letter, and its length is 6.
We can change one character to get the new string "AABBBBA". The substring "BBBB" is the longest with the same character. There are other ways to achieve this result as well.

solution.approach: Use a sliding window approach to track the maximum frequency of a character in the current window and calculate the number of replacements needed to make all characters the same.
